mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.07% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime. Sustained low rates compress equity discount rates and reduce borrowing costs for corporations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of more than 100,000 persons predicts positive equity returns over 60 trading days as sustained hiring supports corporate earnings and consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 100,000 persons month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with robust job creation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) in months following PAYEMS increases > 100k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,421 thousand units signal strong residential construction demand. A sustained level above 1,400 thousand units indicates robust housing market health, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through multiplier effects on construction, materials, and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when HOUST > 1,400k, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 105.583 (2017=100) reflects real economic output capacity. A month-over-month decline in INDPRO of more than 0.5% signals weakening supply-side momentum and future earnings pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and residential demand strength. When both RSAFS grows month-over-month and HOUST remains above 1,350 thousand units, the combined demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures broad-based demand momentum that single-series measures miss.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0% AND HOUST > 1,350 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 619,105 million and HOUST at 1,421 thousand as of 2006-08-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both conditions are met versus when only one is met (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 6.0% combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration. When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a 3-month window while PAYEMS declines, the joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations weaken.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.0% and PAYEMS at 144,120 thousand as of 2006-09-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 264.793 (1982-1984=100) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, the central bank's restrictive stance compresses equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in a tightening regime.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 264.793 and FEDFUNDS at 0.07% as of 2006-09-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both conditions are met versus baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 18,794.426 billion chained 2017 dollars reflects aggregate demand strength. In late-cycle expansion regimes (GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized), positive equity returns persist over 60 trading days as earnings growth remains robust. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it fails in recession or early-cycle phases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% in prior quarter
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with above-trend GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 2.0%) when GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or mechanism reverses to negative returns in recession phases (GDPC1 growth < 0%), indicating regime-dependence failure.
